reasons: Lolita can reduce Valir's poke value and protect teammates during siege or objective setup.
She helps the team walk through Valir's zoning without taking as much chip damage.

proof.summary: Valir wants to repeatedly zone enemies with ranged fireballs and knockback; Lolita can protect allies and create safer forward movement.
worksBestWhen: Lolita saves protection for Valir's poke before objective fights.
Teammates walk behind Lolita instead of splitting into Valir's angles.
failureCases: Valir waits out Lolita's shield and then re-engages.
Lolita is flanked or forced to face the wrong direction.

reasons: Lancelot can dodge Valir's skillshots and reach him from non-linear angles.
Valir is weaker when his knockback or ultimate is forced before the real dive.

proof.summary: Valir controls straight paths well, but Lancelot can use mobility and timing to bypass or dodge the control line.
worksBestWhen: Lancelot waits for Valir's knockback or defensive ultimate to be used.
Lancelot enters from flank paths rather than front lane.
failureCases: Valir's team layers additional crowd control after the knockback.

reasons: Pharsa can outrange Valir's normal control zone during objective fights.
Her artillery forces Valir to reposition instead of freely zoning the frontline.

proof.summary: Valir wants to control mid-range space, while Pharsa can threaten from longer artillery range with Feathered Air Strike.
worksBestWhen: Pharsa casts from outside Valir's reliable knockback range.
The team protects Pharsa from flank divers.
failureCases: Valir's team reaches Pharsa before she finishes ultimate.
Pharsa casts too close and gets knocked away or forced to escape.

reasons: Kagura can poke from flexible angles and reposition around Valir's linear control.
Her escape tools make it harder for Valir to lock her into the knockback path.

proof.summary: Valir punishes predictable forward movement; Kagura can reposition and trade from angles that are harder for Valir to control.
worksBestWhen: Kagura does not spend her escape before Valir uses knockback.
She pokes from side angles, not directly through Valir's fire path.
failureCases: Kagura misuses umbrella position and cannot escape.
Valir saves knockback until Kagura commits.

reasons: Hayabusa can threaten Valir from side lanes and force him to hold defensive cooldowns.
Valir struggles if he is attacked after defensive tools have already been used.

proof.summary: Valir survives dives by pushing enemies away and using defensive timing; Hayabusa can punish when those tools are unavailable.
worksBestWhen: Hayabusa waits out Valir's defensive ultimate.
He attacks when Valir is away from peel.
failureCases: Valir saves knockback for Hayabusa's entry.
Hayabusa's damage spreads across multiple nearby targets.
